title: Lab-Grown Tissue Completes Full Menstrual Cycle - TCR 05/01/26
description:
Friedrich Miescher Institute scientists grew an endometrial organoid that completed a full menstrual cycle in vitro, Apple disclosed Mac Minis will be supply-constrained for months as OpenClaw users buy $600 boxes to host autonomous agents locally, and Spotify launched a Verified badge for human artists after Deezer found 44% of new uploads are AI-generated and 97% of listeners cannot distinguish them.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-may-1-2026/

0:00 House subcommittee cuts NSF by 20%
0:34 Friedrich Miescher endometrial organoid completes menstrual cycle
3:23 Mac Mini shortage and the agentic AI substrate
6:41 The Second Computer
8:54 Spotify Verified badge for human artists
12:43 What shipped
13:45 Arizona lawsuit and the synthetic identity business model

This episode covers the Cell Stem Cell organoid breakthrough, the Mac Mini supply crunch as OpenClaw users move agentic AI onto consumer hardware, Spotify's Verified badge for human artists, the Arizona lawsuit testing the Take It Down Act against a scrape-train-sell business model, the 20% NSF budget cut, and the Venice Biennale jury's collective resignation.

A research substrate unavailable to the entire field of regenerative biology last week is available to thousands of labs this week. The Friedrich Miescher team's endometrial organoid, published in Cell Stem Cell, completed a full menstrual cycle in vitro and identified surface luminal cells as the drivers of scarless regeneration - upending the textbook model and opening a new path for wound healing, fibrosis, and tissue engineering research. Tim Cook disclosed Mac Minis will be supply-constrained for several months as OpenClaw users buy second computers to host autonomous agents locally, dropping the floor of agentic AI ownership to a $600 box. Spotify rolled out a Verified badge distinguishing human artists from AI profiles after Deezer reported 44% of new uploads are AI-generated and 97% of listeners cannot tell the difference. Three Arizona women filed suit alleging a scrape-train-sell business architecture targeting women with under 50,000 followers and selling $24.95/month courses teaching the technique - testing whether the Take It Down Act reaches the playbook, not just the photos. The House subcommittee voted to cut NSF funding by 20% in a single year while Japan, the UK, China, and the EU pour capital into the same basic-science layer. The Venice Biennale jury collectively resigned after the Foundation rejected its position on Russia and Israel. The capability layer is widening at the consumer endpoint while institutions discover their stated values only matter when their structures can carry them.

Additional Reading:
- Nature - This organoid can menstruate and shows how tissue can repair itself: https://www.nature.com/articles/d41586-026-01428-5

- The Guardian - Spotify rolls out 'Verified' badge to distinguish human artists from AI: https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2026/apr/30/spotify-verified-badge-human-artists-from-ai
- Wired - These Men Allegedly Profit Off Teaching People How to Make AI Porn: https://www.wired.com/story/ai-porn-lawsuit-arizona/
- Nature - US lawmakers vote to reduce science spending: https://www.nature.com/articles/d41586-026-01427-6
- Shared Sapience - The Century Report April 30, 2026 (PocketOS Claude database deletion): https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-april-30-2026/
